rulings.Case Title: ROBERT MONTOGMERY VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc. And Hanson Permanente Cement, Inc.S Motion To Abate
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 20, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendants Kaiser Gypsum Co., Inc.¿s and Hanson Permanente Cement, Inc. fka Kaiser Cement Corp.¿s motion to abate is continued to March 23 to provide the plaintiffs with additional time to determine the existence of additional heirs. The trial date of January 24, 2011 is vacated. Supplemental opposition to the motion is due on or before March 14. Supplemental reply papers are due on or before March 18. At the hearing on March 23 the court will entertain: requests 1) to set a new trial date or, in the alternative, put the case on a future status and setting conference calendar and 2) to reopen discovery. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: SAIPELE FAIAIPAU VS. COLTEC INDUSTRIES, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 20, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant J and H Marine & Industrial Engineering Company¿s motion for summary judgment and alternate summary adjudication are denied. Defendant¿s separate statement fails to set forth sufficient facts entitling defendant to any of the relief it seeks. Specifically, the separate statement acknowledges that employees of defendant¿s predecessor worked in the presence of Mr. Faiapau, but nowhere in the separate statement is there any fact establishing the absence of asbestos in any of the materials that those employees worked with. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DONNA O'BALLE VS. ASSOCIATED INSULATION OF CALIFORNIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 20, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5. Defendant Temporary Plant Cleaners, Inc.¿s demurrer to plaintiff¿s complaint is sustained with 10 days leave to amend to give plaintiff an opportunity, if she is able to do so, to allege facts sufficient to meet the requirements of Bockrath and to address the issue raised in the motion to strike. = (220/HEK)
